behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: Classification correctness depends on real AI vision output (explicit-content detection), so mocks can hide failures. The behavior also checks hold-vs-publish side effects, but Workflow is not required because this is a single model-dependent decision, not a multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run real image submissions containing explicit content through the moderation service with the real vision model enabled; assert each submission is flagged, appears in the review-hold store/queue, and is absent from the published feed via readback.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: The core risk is model judgment quality on adversarial/obfuscated text, which unit/integration stubs cannot validate. Workflow is not required because the behavior is one model decision class, not multi-step agentic handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a corpus of hate-speech variants (leet substitutions, Unicode confusables, spacing/punctuation obfuscation) to the real text model path and assert they are flagged for hold/review.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is primarily about externally visible side effect correctness (published vs held/dropped), requiring round-trip observation at the external boundary. Agentic was considered but rejected because the text only requires behavior when checks pass, not validating model output quality; deterministic pass responses can drive this path.
behaviors.test_description: Exercise the moderation API end-to-end with inputs that the check services return as pass; then verify via real readback endpoint/feed that the post is published and absent from hold queues, with no drop.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly a full pipeline SLA across multiple AI-backed stages in sequence (image model step + text model step + final decision). Single-step tests miss cross-step latency accumulation and orchestration effects.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end submissions through the real pipeline path and measure submission-to-final-decision latency across repeated runs at representative peak-like concurrency; assert p95 (or required percentile) is <= 5 seconds.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: This is external error-path handling for a real dependency outage, which requires verifying real failure contract/timeout behavior and observable retry-queue side effects. Agentic was considered but rejected because no model-output-quality judgment is involved: the behavior text is specifically 'model unavailable'.
behaviors.test_description: Induce real vision-dependency unavailability (e.g., endpoint unreachable/timeout) in an end-to-end run; assert posts are not approved, retry jobs are created, and queue state is visible via readback/metrics.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: Pass/fail depends on consistency across multiple model-dependent submissions over time (cross-step state/decision stability), not a single isolated classification. Agentic single-call checks are insufficient because they do not verify sequence-level consistency.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image repeatedly within the target window through the real moderation path; assert decisions remain consistent across attempts and that any cache/stability policy is honored end-to-end.
